name: AP Pizza Shop Bethesda

notes: AP Pizza Shop's current restaurant-linked Toast catalog is meal-period dependent: the lunch surface publishes 46 products and the dinner surface publishes 35, yielding 49 unique current products across seven categories.
All 47 frozen rows were reconciled: 37 exact matches, one renamed variant, seven stale products, and two Toast category-heading artifacts. The 38 matched products leave eleven current omissions.
The repair publishes the complete 49-product union, including Duke #7, Pizza Kit, and Pizza Dough from dinner, without duplicating the 32 products shared by current meal periods.

The current restaurant-linked Toast item surfaces publish selected ingredient descriptions but no complete allergen matrix or cross-contact disclosure. Forty-six products have supported positive signals; three remain accurately unavailable.

Twenty-seven matched frozen allergen outcomes differ from the corrected evidence. The repair adds omitted pizza/focaccia/pasta wheat and gluten, direct dairy, sesame, egg, tree-nut, and fish signals without treating absence as safety.

AP Caesar explicitly lists anchovy dressing. The Tripper explicitly lists neonata; a separately labeled third-party culinary reference establishes neonata as whitebait/fish but is not promoted to official allergen evidence.
The retained Jina Reader artifacts are transport corroboration only and are absent from app source URLs.

The frozen output was a stale mixed meal-period snapshot that promoted Deck-Oven Slices and Lunch Pies headings into products, retained seven superseded rows, omitted eleven current products, and flattened 31 matched presentations into the Pizza category or stale descriptions.

The reviewed parser recovers 46 lunch products from stable Toast item URLs, explicitly adds only the three independently observed dinner-only products, canonicalizes regular and 18-inch pies by reviewed identity, and asserts a 49-product seven-category union.

Focused tests cover both meal-period counts, every frozen-row disposition, current omissions, stable source-authority boundaries, neonata semantics, published repair invariants, target-only isolation, and idempotency.

sourceAttempts.outcome: HTTP 200. Confirmed the current All-Purpose owner site and its AP Pizza Shop Bethesda identity.
HTTP 403 for the ledger archival user-agent; no official artifact was recorded from the failed request.
The public web reader returned the live restaurant-linked dinner surface with 35 products, including dinner-only Duke #7, Pizza Kit, and Pizza Dough.
The public web reader returned the live restaurant-linked lunch surface with all 46 products and stable item URLs.
HTTP 200. Retained a 19,446-byte third-party transport snapshot containing the 46 current lunch product anchors and Toast item URLs.
HTTP 200. The direct Duke #7 route returned a 19,495-byte scheduled lunch surface, corroborating Toast's meal-period routing but not serving as dinner authority.
HTTP 200. Retained a third-party culinary reference explaining that whitebait on Italian restaurant menus is called neonata; used only to interpret the official ingredient term as fish.
Targeted review of the restaurant-linked lunch and dinner surfaces found ingredient descriptions but no allergen matrix, may-contain statement, shared-equipment disclosure, or negative assurance.

findings.summary: The Toast catalog changes by meal period; treating one surface as the complete menu omits current lunch or dinner products.
findings.resolution: Reconciled the 46-product lunch and 35-product dinner surfaces into a reviewed 49-product unique union.

findings.summary: The frozen catalog retained seven stale products, promoted two headings, and omitted eleven current products.
findings.resolution: Removed stale/artifact rows and restored every product present on either current meal-period surface.

findings.summary: Twenty-seven matched frozen products had missing or overstated positive signals relative to current names, categories, and ingredient descriptions.
findings.resolution: Recomputed positive signals from the current item evidence, left three products unavailable, and invented no negative/cross-contact claim.

findings.summary: The Tripper lists neonata, a whitebait/fish ingredient that the frozen extraction failed to recognize.
findings.resolution: Mapped neonata to fish while preserving the boundary between restaurant-linked occurrence evidence and third-party ingredient semantics.

findings.summary: The archival user-agent receives HTTP 403, requiring third-party transport artifacts to retain the full lunch surface.
findings.resolution: Anchored product claims to independently read live Toast pages/item URLs, labeled Jina captures third-party, and excluded Jina URLs from app data.

repairs.summary: Rebuilt AP Pizza Shop's current meal-period union and corrected stale/artifact rows, omissions, category/description associations, allergen semantics, and source authority boundaries.
